About Prudential International School

Who We Are

A world-class institution in the heart of Gwarinpa, Abuja — shaping minds, building character, and making a difference since 2014.

Est. 2014 · Gwarinpa, Abuja

Making a Difference in Every Child's Future

Prudential International School (PIS) is a premier private school at Plot 18, 2nd Avenue, Gwarinpa Estate, Abuja. We offer world-class education from Nursery through Senior Secondary School, nurturing every child's unique potential.

Our school crest — featuring a soaring eagle — embodies our belief that every student can rise to greatness with the right guidance, environment, and determination.

With over 1,200 enrolled students, a 97% WAEC pass rate, and 11+ years of excellence, PIS stands as one of Abuja's foremost centres of academic and character development.

🏭 Our History

  • Founded in 2014 in Gwarinpa, Abuja with a vision to redefine quality education
  • Started with Nursery and Primary classes; expanded to full Secondary in 2018
  • First WAEC cohort achieved 97% pass rate, setting the standard for all future classes
  • Introduced Ballet, STEM Labs, and the Arts Fiesta programme by 2020
  • Recognised among Abuja's top private schools three years running
  • Opened a dedicated Science & Innovation Centre in 2023

🏮 Our Facilities

  • Fully equipped Physics, Chemistry and Biology laboratories
  • Modern ICT & Computing lab with high-speed internet
  • 3,000-volume library with digital research access
  • Dedicated Arts & Music studio
  • Professional basketball court and football pitch
  • Ballet and dance studio with sprung wooden floor
  • Air-conditioned classrooms with interactive whiteboards
🏆
Accreditation & Recognition

Prudential International School is fully accredited by the Federal Ministry of Education and the Abuja Education Board. Our curriculum aligns with both the Nigerian Educational Research and Development Council (NERDC) and British international standards, giving our students a truly global academic foundation.

🎯

Our Mission

To provide world-class, holistic education that nurtures intellectual curiosity, moral integrity, and creative leadership.

🦅

Our Vision

To be the foremost centre of academic excellence in West Africa, producing confident, compassionate citizens.

Science Excellence at PIS

Our state-of-the-art laboratories give every student hands-on experience with real scientific equipment from an early age. Students develop critical thinking, precision, and a genuine love of discovery — hallmarks of a PIS education.

Our Science programme has produced regional competition winners and students who go on to study Medicine, Engineering, and the Sciences at top Nigerian and international universities.

📈 Our School Journey

From a small but ambitious nursery school in 2015 to one of Abuja's most comprehensive international institutions, Prudential International School has grown steadily — driven by a passionate team, dedicated parents, and exceptionally talented students. Every year, we raise the bar.

2015 — Founded

Opened with Nursery 1–3 and Primary 1–2. First 80 students enrolled. Mission: Make a difference.

2017 — Primary School Fully Established

Primary 1–6 classes operational. STEM laboratory opened. Chess and Scrabble clubs launched.

2018 — Junior Secondary School Launched

JSS 1–3 introduced. Science in Action annual event held for the first time. 400+ students enrolled.

2020 — Ballet & Arts Programmes

Professional Ballet studio opened. Annual Arts Fiesta launched as a flagship community event.

2021 — First WAEC Results

SSS 3 debut sitting: 97% distinction rate. PIS placed in top tier of Abuja secondary schools.

2023 — Innovation Centre & Basketball Court

World-class basketball court inaugurated. ICT lab upgraded with 40 new workstations.

Today — 1,200+ Students & Growing

Full Crèche-to-College pipeline. 50+ qualified teachers. Multiple national competition winners.

Academic Programmes

Education for Every Stage

From Nursery to Senior Secondary — a seamless, rigorous, and enriching academic journey.

🌿
Crèche & Nursery
Ages 6 months–5 yrs. Montessori-inspired, play-based, sensorial exploration in a safe nurturing environment.
📚
Primary School
Primary 1–6. British-Nigerian curriculum blend with STEM integration, project-based learning and co-curriculars.
🎓
Secondary School
JSS 1 – SSS 3. Rigorous WAEC/NECO prep, specialist teachers, science labs and university counselling.

University & Career Counselling

From JSS 3 onwards, every student has access to our dedicated university counselling office. We guide students and families through course selection, JAMB preparation, direct entry applications, and international university admissions — including UK, Canada, and USA pathways. Our alumni are studying in universities across four continents.

👥 Co-Curricular Excellence

At PIS, education extends far beyond the classroom. Our structured co-curricular programme includes weekly sessions in Chess, Scrabble, Ballet, Football, Graphic Design, Speech & Debate, and Science Club — all led by trained specialist coaches. Students who participate consistently in co-curricular activities at PIS demonstrate measurably higher academic performance and social confidence.

Admission Checklist

Admissions Process

How to Enrol Your Child

Our admissions process is straightforward, transparent, and designed to be stress-free for families.

  1. Submit Application Form

    Download or collect the Admission Application Form from our Admin office or website. Complete all sections and return with a non-refundable application fee of ₦5,000.

  2. Gather Required Documents

    Birth certificate, last school report card, passport photographs ×4, valid parent ID, proof of residence, vaccination records, and a letter of good conduct from the previous school.

  3. Entrance Assessment

    All applicants from Primary 2 upwards sit a short entrance assessment in English Language and Mathematics to determine appropriate class placement. Nursery applicants attend an informal observation session.

  4. Receive Admission Letter

    Within 5 working days of assessment, successful applicants receive an official Offer of Admission letter specifying the class, term fees, and commencement date.

  5. Pay First Term Fees & Collect Welcome Pack

    Complete tuition payment, collect your child's school timetable, uniforms information, school handbook, and sign the Parent-School Partnership Agreement.

📅 2025/2026 Term Dates

  • First Term: September 15 – December 13, 2025
  • Christmas Holiday: December 14, 2025 – January 11, 2026
  • Second Term: January 12 – April 4, 2026
  • Easter Holiday: April 5 – April 26, 2026
  • Third Term: April 27 – July 25, 2026
  • Long Holiday: July 26 – September 14, 2026

💰 Fee Payment Information

  • Fees are due before or on the first day of each term
  • Payment by bank transfer, POS, or cash at the Admin office
  • Bank: GTBank — Prudential International School
  • Account: 0123456789 (collect current details from Admin)
  • Receipt issued immediately upon payment confirmation
  • Instalment plans available on request — speak to the Bursar
  • Sibling discount: 10% on second child's tuition fee
Frequently Asked Questions

Parents Ask, We Answer

Click any question to reveal the answer.

What is the school's admission age for the Nursery? ⌄
We accept children from 18 months into our Crèche programme and from 2 years into Nursery 1. Children must turn the required age within the academic year of admission.
Does PIS offer scholarships or bursaries? ⌄
Yes. PIS offers merit-based scholarships to exceptional students entering JSS 1 and SSS 1. Applications open each May. We also have a needs-based bursary fund — speak confidentially with the Bursar or Principal for details.
Are uniforms compulsory every day? ⌄
Full school uniform is mandatory Monday to Friday. Sports kit is worn on designated PE days only. The full uniform package — including school shirt, trousers/skirt, tie, and school bag — can be purchased from our Admin office.
How do I track my child's academic progress? ⌄
PIS provides a digital parent portal where you can view weekly assessment scores, attendance records, and teacher remarks. In addition, we hold formal Parent-Teacher Conference days at the end of every term. Report cards are issued at end-of-term.
Is there a school bus service? ⌄
Yes. Our school bus service covers Gwarinpa, Kubwa, Life Camp, Jabi, Wuse, and surrounding areas. Routes and pick-up times are issued at the start of each term. The bus fee is ₦45,000 per term. Please contact the Admin office to confirm your route coverage.
Does PIS have a lunch programme? ⌄
Yes. Our school cafeteria serves nutritious, freshly prepared Nigerian and continental meals daily. The lunch programme costs ₦35,000 per term and includes a mid-morning snack and a full hot lunch. A weekly menu is shared with parents every Friday.
What safety measures does PIS have in place? ⌄
The school is fully perimeter-fenced with 24-hour security personnel and CCTV coverage. All visitors must sign in at the gatehouse. Children are only released to pre-authorised individuals. We have a trained school nurse on campus every day and a clear emergency response protocol.
Important Notice: Fee amounts shown in the estimator above are indicative and subject to review at the start of each academic year. Official fee schedules are issued with the Admission Offer Letter. For the most current figures, please contact our Admin office on +234 809 570 0591 or email pis.abuja@gmail.com.

Beyond the Classroom

Student Life at PIS

At Prudential International School, education is a full, rich experience. Discover the clubs, events, traditions, and community that make PIS truly special.

Est. 2014 · Gwarinpa, Abuja

A Vibrant, Nurturing School Community

From the moment a child joins Prudential, they become part of a warm, ambitious family. Our students don't just attend school — they belong to something greater: a community built on shared values, friendly competition, creative expression, and mutual support.

Every day at PIS is an opportunity to discover a new talent, make a lifelong friend, or achieve something you never thought possible.

🏃 Co-Curricular Activities & Clubs
Students are encouraged to explore their passions through our structured weekly club sessions — all led by trained specialist coaches.
♞

Chess & Scrabble Club

Every Tuesday · All levels
  • Weekly coaching sessions with certified instructors
  • Inter-school tournament participation annually
  • Regional Championship winners 2022, 2023, 2024
  • National Scrabble Competition top-3 finishers
  • Open to Primary 4 – SSS 3
🔬

STEM & Science Club

Every Wednesday · JSS–SSS
  • Hands-on lab experiments beyond the curriculum
  • Annual "Science in Action" showcase event
  • Robotics and coding introductions
  • Guest lectures from industry professionals
  • Nigeria Science Olympiad preparation
🦰

Ballet & Dance

Every Thursday · All ages
  • Professional ballet studio with sprung wooden floor
  • Certified ballet instructor on staff
  • Annual end-of-year dance recital
  • Open to Nursery 2 through SSS 3
  • Costume and performance opportunities
⚽

Football & Sports

Every Friday · Primary–SSS
  • Full-size artificial turf football pitch
  • Professional basketball court available daily
  • Athletics, relay races & field events
  • Inter-house Sports Day held annually
  • School teams participate in Abuja league fixtures
🎤

Speech, Drama & Debate

Every Monday · JSS–SSS
  • Weekly elocution and public speaking sessions
  • Annual drama production and talent showcase
  • Inter-school debate competitions
  • Confidence-building and presentation skills
  • Mock UN and current affairs discussions
🎨

Graphic Design & Arts

Every Tuesday · Primary–SSS
  • Digital design tools and traditional art media
  • Annual Arts Fiesta exhibition for families
  • Portfolio development for secondary students
  • Photography and visual storytelling projects
  • Collaboration with local galleries and artists
🎉 Annual Events & Traditions
Every school year at PIS is punctuated by memorable events that students and families look forward to.
September · Term 1
🎓 Resumption & Welcome Assembly
New students are warmly welcomed in a whole-school assembly. Class prefects are announced, clubs open for registration, and the academic calendar is shared with all families.
October · Term 1
🌐 PIS Cultural Day
Students celebrate Nigeria's rich cultural diversity — dressed in traditional attire from across the country's 36 states. Traditional dances, food fair, and cultural exhibitions fill the school grounds.
November · Term 1
🔬 Science in Action Day
Our flagship STEM showcase event where students from Primary 4 to SSS 3 present science projects, conduct live experiments, and demonstrate innovations to parents, judges, and invited guests.
February · Term 2
⚽ Inter-House Sports Day
The biggest sporting event of the year — four houses compete across athletics, football, basketball, relay races, and field events. Parents and alumni fill the stands for a full day of thrilling competition and school spirit.
March · Term 2
🎨 Annual Arts Fiesta
A week-long celebration of visual arts, music, dance, drama, and spoken word. Students exhibit original artworks, perform for families, and celebrate creative excellence. One of PIS's most beloved traditions.
June · Term 3
🎓 Graduation & Prize Giving Ceremony
We celebrate our graduating JSS 3 and SSS 3 students with a formal graduation ceremony, prize giving, and valedictory service. Top academic performers, sports stars, and all-round students are publicly honoured.
January · New Year
📆 PIS Open Day
Prospective families are welcomed for a guided campus tour, Q&A with teachers and the Principal, student-led demonstrations, and an opportunity to experience PIS firsthand. Held every January.
"Let's give your wonderful kids the roots to grow and wings to fly."
— Prudential International School · Making a Difference

Every student belongs to one of four houses, competing for the prestigious House Shield throughout the year.

🎓 Prefect & Leadership System
PIS invests deeply in developing student leaders — our prefects serve as role models and bridges between the student body and school management.
👑
Head Boy
Senior male prefect. Elected by students and staff. Chairs the Student Council and represents students at school events.
👒
Head Girl
Senior female prefect. Elected by students and staff. Co-chairs the Student Council and mentors junior students.
🏁
House Captains
One captain per house. Organises house members for sports, events, and community service. Earns house points.
👥
Class Prefects
Elected in each class from JSS 1 upwards. Assists teachers, maintains order, and represents classmates at council.

  • White shirt with school crest embroidered on pocket
  • Navy blue trousers (boys) / Navy blue pleated skirt (girls)
  • School tie — navy with gold stripe (Senior) / Red tartan (Junior)
  • Red school sweater/vest for cold weather
  • Black polished leather shoes and white socks
  • School bag with PIS logo — compulsory
  • Full PE kit on designated sports days

🏀 Sports & Recreation

  • Full-size artificial turf football pitch with floodlights
  • Professional indoor/outdoor basketball court
  • Athletics track for sprint and relay events
  • Ballet and dance studio with sprung floor
  • Table tennis, badminton and indoor games room
  • Dedicated PE periods twice weekly for all classes
  • Annual inter-school sports competitions

🤝 Student Welfare & Support
We care deeply about the whole child — academic, emotional, physical, and social wellbeing.

💊 Health & Medical Care

  • Trained school nurse on campus every school day
  • First-aid facility with basic medical supplies
  • Emergency response protocol for all staff
  • Health records maintained for every student
  • Annual health and vision screening for all classes
  • Partnership with nearby clinic for urgent referrals

💡 Counselling & Support

  • Dedicated student welfare officer
  • Confidential one-on-one counselling sessions
  • Anti-bullying programme and peer mediation
  • Learning support for students with special needs
  • Parent-counsellor meetings available on request
  • Career guidance from JSS 3 through SSS 3

🍽 Nutrition & Lunch Programme

  • Freshly prepared hot meals served daily in the cafeteria
  • Nigerian and continental menu options weekly
  • Mid-morning healthy snack for all students
  • Nutritionist-reviewed menus shared with parents
  • Clean filtered drinking water available all day
  • Lunch programme: ₦35,000 per term (optional)

🔒 Safety & Security

  • Fully perimeter-fenced campus with 24-hour guards
  • CCTV coverage across all school zones
  • All visitors must sign in at the security gatehouse
  • Students released only to pre-authorised persons
  • Fire drills and emergency evacuations each term
  • Safe school transport routes with GPS-tracked buses
Science in Action — PIS Laboratory

Every student at PIS gets real hands-on laboratory time from Primary 4 onwards — building the foundation for careers in Medicine, Engineering, and the Sciences.

👥 Parent & Community Involvement

At PIS, parents are active partners in education. Our Parent-Teacher Association (PTA) meets every term, contributing to school development, event planning, and policy feedback. Parents receive weekly updates via our digital portal, termly report cards, and a formal Parent-Teacher Conference at the end of each term. We also host an annual PIS Open Day every January — welcoming prospective families and celebrating our community.

Discipline & Order

School Rules & Guidelines

Our code of conduct ensures a safe, orderly, and productive learning environment.

Code of Conduct

1
Students must greet all staff and visitors respectfully at all times.
2
Bullying, verbal abuse, or harassment results in immediate disciplinary action.
3
Mobile phones are prohibited within school premises during school hours.
4
Students must participate actively in all scheduled classes and events.
5
Vandalism of school property attracts full replacement cost and suspension.
6
Academic dishonesty — including plagiarism — is a suspendable offence.

Dress Code

1
Full school uniform must be worn on all academic days — clean, pressed, and correctly labelled.
2
Hair must be kept neat and natural. Coloured hair or elaborate styles are not permitted.
3
Plain white or navy socks with black polished shoes are mandatory.
4
Sports kit is only worn on designated Physical Education days.
5
Jewellery, nail polish, and makeup are not permitted for any student.

Punctuality & Attendance

1
School gates open at 7:00 AM and close at 7:45 AM. Late arrivals require a signed parent note.
2
A student missing more than 15% of a term classes may be required to repeat the term.
3
All absences exceeding two days must be accompanied by a medical certificate.
4
Early departure requires written parental authorisation submitted to the Admin office.
5
The school runs Monday to Friday, 7:45 AM to 3:30 PM.

🎯 Why Discipline Matters at PIS

  • Discipline creates the calm, focused environment where learning flourishes
  • Our rules are designed to be fair, consistent, and age-appropriate
  • Every rule has a clear rationale explained to students at the start of term
  • Restorative justice — not just punishment — is central to our approach
  • The Student Council gives pupils a formal voice in shaping school norms

⚔ Disciplinary Process

  • Step 1: Verbal warning and counselling by class teacher
  • Step 2: Written warning sent to parent/guardian
  • Step 3: Meeting with Vice-Principal and parent
  • Step 4: Suspension pending behaviour review
  • Step 5: Expulsion (reserved for severe or repeated offences)

👥 Student Council & Prefect System

PIS believes every student deserves a voice. Our elected Student Council — comprising the Head Boy, Head Girl, House Captains, and class representatives — meets fortnightly with the Principal to discuss school life, propose improvements, and resolve student concerns. The Prefect system trains tomorrow's leaders in responsibility, empathy, and servant leadership. Nominations open each September for JSS 3 and SSS students.

📞
Report a Concern

If your child has experienced or witnessed bullying, harassment, or any breach of the school's code of conduct, please contact our Welfare Officer directly on +234 906 421 9878 or email pis.abuja@gmail.com. All reports are handled confidentially and with care. The safety and wellbeing of every student is our highest priority.
